patch.value: Complete scaffold capability matrix: --capability renderers/parser/preflight/services starters

Follow-up to pm-d1ig (schema). After schema lands, the scaffold supports 5 of 9 SDK capabilities (commands/hooks/search/importers/schema). Add starters for the four override surfaces so 'pm package init --capability' covers all 9: renderers (api.registerRenderer toon|json output override), parser (api.registerParser argv rewrite), preflight (api.registerPreflight runtime-decision delta), services (api.registerService named service override). Each has define*/assert*/run* SDK helpers already. These are GLOBAL-activation capabilities (renderers/parser/preflight in GLOBAL_EXTENSION_ACTIVATION_CAPABILITIES; services conservative), so unlike schema they activate appropriately. Defer until schema proven; override starters need careful design to be genuinely useful rather than noise.

pm package/extension init --capability renderers|parser|preflight|services each produce a type-checking, test-passing starter that registers the matching SDK override surface; scaffold now covers all 9 SDK capabilities; 100% coverage; docs/completion/help updated; no regression to the other five capabilities.

Added the four override-surface scaffold capabilities, completing the matrix at 9 of 9 SDK capabilities (commands/hooks/search/importers/schema/renderers/parser/preflight/services). renderers registers a toon api.registerRenderer override scoped to its own command (returns null to pass other output through); parser registers an api.registerParser override that rewrites the command's parsed options (deprecated-alias rewrite); preflight registers an api.registerPreflight override over the pre-run migration/format gate decision (safe no-op echo); services overrides the built-in output_format service via api.registerService (scoped to its own command). Each emits a runnable node:test using the matching assert*/run* helpers (assertRegistered{Renderer,Parser,Preflight,Service}Override + runRegistered{Renderer,Parser,Preflight,Service}OverrideForTest) and README define* guidance (defineRendererOverride/defineParserOverride/definePreflightOverride/defineServiceOverride). KEY activation design (extends pm-halx): renderers/parser/preflight are GLOBAL-tier so they declare activation.commands=[ping] for lazy command dispatch while their override surface activates regardless; services is CONSERVATIVE-tier but its override is command-scoped, so it ALSO declares activation.commands=[ping] (lazy) rather than omitting it like schema — only schema (a truly global item-type contribution) omits the field. Verified in temp workspaces: generated node:test 3/3 pass for all four, tsc --noEmit exit 0, real install + ping dispatch works, renderer/service overrides fire for the own command and pass through pm list output. Full suite 248 files / 5013 tests at 100/100/100/100 coverage; static-quality and docs-skills gates green.

Completed the scaffold capability matrix at 9 of 9 SDK capabilities by adding renderers/parser/preflight/services starters. Each generates a type-checking, test-passing starter that registers the matching SDK override surface (api.registerRenderer/registerParser/registerPreflight/registerService) with a runnable node:test using the assert*/run* helpers and README define* guidance. Activation tiers handled per pm-halx: renderers/parser/preflight (GLOBAL) and services (CONSERVATIVE, command-scoped) all declare activation.commands=[ping] for lazy dispatch; only schema omits the field. Verified end-to-end in temp workspaces (3/3 node:test each, tsc clean, real install + override behavior + non-disruptive pass-through). Docs (EXTENSIONS.md, SDK.md), help-content, and shell completion updated; 5013 tests at 100% coverage.

Verified end-to-end in temp workspaces (3/3 node:test each, tsc clean, real install + override behavior + non-disruptive pass-through of pm list). Docs (EXTENSIONS.md, SDK.md), help-content, and shell completion updated; 5013 tests at 100% coverage.
